steps.title: Find the one step multiplier
steps.content: The function is $z(w)=(0.836)^{2w}$. When $w$ increases by $1$, the exponent $2w$ increases by $2$, so the output is multiplied by the base twice: $$(0.836)^2=0.698896$$

steps.title: Convert to a decrease
steps.content: A multiplier of $0.698896$ means the function keeps about $69.89\%$ of its previous value for each one-unit increase in $w$. The decrease is the part missing from $1$: $$1-0.698896=0.301104$$

steps.title: Choose the closest percent
steps.content: As a percent, $0.301104$ is about $30.1\%$. The smaller choices come from using $0.836$ directly or confusing the base with the percent decrease, but the exponent changes by $2$ each step. The answer is D.
